calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Motion For Order Granting Relief From Dismissal
Off Calendar Apr-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jin H. Kim, Esq., from Sparer Law Group, 415-217-7300, for defendant and opposing party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFF JUDITH BERRY's Motion For Order Granting Relief From Dismissal is Denied. CCP 473(b) does not permit relief from deadlines deemed jurisdictional in nature, such as filing of a timely notice of motion for a new trial. Order signed in open court. Judge: James J. McBride; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Plaintiff Judith Berry'S Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Sep-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JUDITH TUCEVICH BERRY'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL.

APPEARANCES: Alan W. Sparer, Esq. of Sparer Law Group 217-7300 for deft & opposing party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENY. MOTION UNTIMELY PER CCP 659 & 660. PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE THE NOTICE OF INTENT TO MOVE FOR NEW TRIAL IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 659. IT IS ALSO TOO LATE TO HAVE A HEARING PER CCP 660. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Nte Of Hearing; Mtn For New Trial
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JUDITH BERRY'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued to September 19, 2011. Moving party to provide a courtesy copy of motion to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison, CSR #4609
